mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following the trigger, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening employment conditions and rising aggregate wages, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >50% of months following the CPI threshold breach, or the signal loses predictive power when core inflation remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP combined with a declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative policy supporting economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when GDP growth exceeds 2% and rates fall, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside falling industrial production during a late-cycle downturn confirms weakening labor and manufacturing sectors, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration fails to predict negative quarterly returns, or the mechanism produces false signals during supply-side shocks where unemployment remains stable.
